summary: npmkekw ships base64-decoded TCP payload executor
details: Package npmkekw@2.0.3 contains misc/tcp.js, which uses Buffer.from(..., 'base64').toString(...) to decode obfuscated payloads in a file whose name ("tcp.js") indicates raw TCP networking. The package has no documented purpose justifying a base64-decoded TCP component shipped under a misc/ directory, and the obfuscation-and-decode pattern in conjunction with raw socket code is the canonical shape of a remote-code-execution / command-and-control loader. There is no legitimate functional reason for an npm module to ship base64-encoded blobs that are decoded inside a TCP handler; the combination is a backdoor/exfil loader fingerprint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
